query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research and provided the structured findings. Here's a summary of what I found on CDC Travel Health Notices for North America:

**Key findings:**

1. **Currently active North America THNs (July 2026):** 5 — Diphtheria in Haiti (New, June 25, 2026), Hepatitis A in Canada/Manitoba (New, June 4, 2026), Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in Northern Mexico (Dec 23, 2025), Oropouche in the Americas incl. Cuba & Panama (Sept 18, 2025), and Salmonella Newport in Mexico (May 30, 2024).

2. **Additional 2025–2026 notices since removed/archived:** A dedicated "Chikungunya in Cuba" Level 2 notice was posted and removed; New World Screwworm across Mexico/Central America drove a CDC HAN advisory (Jan 20, 2026); and Hurricane Melissa (Oct 2025) triggered flood health warnings across the Caribbean.

3. **Frequency:** Counting distinct dedicated North-America THNs, roughly **5–6 per year (~0.4–0.5 per month, one every ~2 months)**. Including removed notices, HAN advisories, hurricane alerts, and global-notice updates touching the region, the effective rate rises to **~0.7–1.0 notice-events per month**.

**Important caveat:** CDC does not publish an official regional "notices per month" statistic, so these rates are derived by counting dated active and archived notices. Activity is seasonally elevated during summer–fall (mosquito-borne disease peaks and Atlantic hurricane season). The active-list snapshot undercounts historical issuance because notices are removed once outbreaks subside.

sources.title: Travel Health Notices | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: Full current list of active CDC Travel Health Notices with levels and posting/update dates, including Diphtheria in Haiti (New June 25, 2026), Hepatitis A in Canada (New June 4, 2026), Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in Mexico (Dec 23, 2025), and Oropouche in the Americas (Sept 18, 2025).

sources.snippet: A Level 2 Chikungunya in Cuba Travel Health Notice was posted and subsequently removed/archived.

sources.snippet: CDC Health Alert Network advisory CDCHAN-00526 distributed January 20, 2026 regarding New World screwworm animal cases in Tamaulipas, Mexico.

sources.title: Mexico - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: New World screwworm myiasis cases reported in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, and Panama with traveler precautions.

sources.title: Jamaica - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: Destination-level flood health warning after Hurricane Melissa affecting Jamaica and several Caribbean destinations.

sources.title: Cuba's Chikungunya Crisis Poses Risks for Visitors
sources.snippet: Reporting on Cuba's 2025 chikungunya outbreak dated December 14, 2025.

sources.title: Chikungunya Outbreaks Span Four Continents as CDC Updates Travel Alerts
sources.snippet: CDC maintains Level 2 Travel Health Notices for Bolivia, Mayotte, Suriname, and Seychelles per April 16, 2026 risk summary.

sources.title: CDC warns about New World screwworm just across the border in Mexico | CIDRAP
sources.snippet: CDC issued a Health Alert Network advisory on New World screwworm animal cases in Tamaulipas, Mexico (January 2026).

data_points.metric: Total active global CDC Travel Health Notices
Distinct dedicated North-America THNs posted in ~13-month window (mid-2025 to July 2026)
Typical rate of new dedicated North-America-affecting THNs (low estimate)
Effective rate incl. removed notices, HAN advisories, hurricane alerts, global-notice updates (high estimate)
New global CDC notices posted in June 2026 (new only, all regions)
